english: — sems-nyid (sems nyid) Glr., Thgy., 'the very soul', but this is often nearly the same as 'spirit', and in the language of the N. T. it may fitly be used for πνεῦμα, and ťugs-nyíd (thugs nyid) for πνεῦμα ἅγιον, Holy Spirit. — sems - rtén (sems rten) keepsake, token Pth. and col. — sems-dón (sems don) an intellectual or spiritual good, gift, or possession Mil. — sems-bdé (sems bde) cheerful, merry Mil. — sems-nád (sems nad) heart-grief, affliction, séms-kyi (sems kyi) nád-du (nad du) č̌e (che) he has much heart-grief Glr. — sems-dpá (sems dpa') a brave mind; byaṅ-č̌ub-sems-dpá (byang chub sems dpa') v. byaṅ-č̌ub (byang chub); sems-dpa-čén-po (sems dpa' chen po), महासत्त्व, a frq. apposition to it. — séms-tsam (sems tsam) a mere thought, idea Was. (134), séms-tsam-pa (sems tsam pa) (Cs.: योगाचार्य) a mystic Köpp. II, 25. — sems-ťsér (sems tsher) fatigue, weariness, disgust Sch.

pf. sems (sems), bsams (bsams), fut. bsam (bsam), imp. som (som), W. *sám-če* (*sam che*), to think, ºdi snyám-du séms-so ('di snyam du sems so) or bsáms-so (bsams so) he thought as follows, he had the following thoughts; lóg-par sém-pa (log par sem pa) to think ill (of a person) Dzl.; to meditate, muse, ponder, sém-bźin-du (sem bzhin du) absorbed in meditation, lost in thought Dzl., mi-dgá-bar sém-śiṅ (mi dga' bar sem shing) immersed in melancholy thoughts Dzl.; in C. *sém-źin-du* (*sem zhin du*) signifies at the present time: knowingly, wilfully, purposely, = śes bźin - du (shes bzhin du); śin-tu sóms śig (shin tu soms shig) think over it seriously! Dzl.; to think of, c. accus., gráṅ-bai gnas (grang ba'i gnas) (to think) of a cool place, i. e. to long for coolness Dzl., and c. dat.: ráṅ-gi yúl-la ma bsám-par (rang gi yul la ma bsam par) forgetful of home, forgetting one's native soil Glr., yi-ge ºdi-la ma bsám-par (yi ge 'di la ma bsam par) disregarding this contract Glr.; also with termin.: gźán-du ma sems śig (gzhan du ma sems shig) do not think of anybody else; to intend, purpose, have in view, e.g. gnód-par byá - bar (gnod par bya bar) to do harm Dzl.; construed in the same manner, it also signifies: to fancy, imagine Do.; with daṅ ºdrá-bar (dang 'dra bar) and similar expressions: to hold, think, consider, to take for, to look upon as; da ºkór-bai nyes-dmigs bsám-śes-na (da 'khor ba'i nyes dmigs bsam shes na) (for bsám-źiṅ (bsam zhing)) now that you know with full consciousness the punishment of (going through) the cycle (of animal existences) Mil. (yet cf. bsám-śes (bsam shes) in bsám-pa (bsam pa)). Sometimes it denotes only an act of memory, a remembering: lhá-čos ťos-bsam-byéd-pa-rnams (lha chos thos bsam byed pa rnams) those who have heard and kept in their memory the religion of Buddha, (who remember the words even without understanding them) Mil. Cf. bsám-pa (bsam pa).
corruption, putrefaction? *már-la sér-ru gyab* (*mar la ser ru gyab*) C. the butter turns yellow and rancid, sér-čan (ser can) rancid S.g.

1. a cleft, slit, fissure, crevice, gap, brag-sér (brag ser) chasm or cleft in a rock; rgya-sér (rgya ser) a large gap, cleft, chasm; sér-ka súb-pa (ser ka sub pa) to close, stop up a hole Pth. — 2. v. sér-po (ser po).
Lamas and laymen, *ser-kyá kuṇ ºdúm-ma ǰhé-pa* (*ser skya kun 'dum ma jhe pa*) a promiscuous convention, parish council C. — 2. v. skyá-bo (skya bo).
a sect of Lamas = dbón-po (dbon po).
Lt. a yellow aquatic flower; *ser-čéṇ* (*ser chen*) W. Saxifraga flagellaris.
avarice, frq.; sér-sna byéd-pa (ser sna byed pa) to be avaricious Dzl.
yellow; ser-ºpréṅ (ser 'phreng) clerical procession, parade Mil. nt.
hail; ser-král (ser khral) a kind of insurance against damage done by hail, i.e. money paid to the Lama for his preventive ceremonies.
1. C. col. finger. — 2. W. six-rowed barley, late barley. — sér-mo-ba (ser mo ba) the Lamas Sch.
1. discord, dissension, naṅ-sél (nang sel) domestic dispute. — 2. a kind of incantation, like brtad (brtad), sel ºjúg-pa (sel 'jug pa) to exorcise, to make use of conjurations or incantations Mil.

pf., fut. bsal (bsal), imp. sol (sol), to remove, esp. impurities, hence to cleanse; to pick, pick off; to blot out, cross out, bú-lon (bu lon) a debt; to clear, *lam sál-če* (*lam sal che*) W. to make a path or road; very frq. fig.: to remove, to remedy (an evil), to cure (a disease), to repair (a damage), to redress grievances), to dispel (darkness) etc.
